name: Saiki - Roll (of Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil milk and allow to cool to lukewarm.
2. In a small bowl, mix yeast with 2 tbsp flour, 1/2 tsp sugar, and 2-3 tbsp warm water. Let rest in a warm place for 10-15 minutes.
3. In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, and sugar. Add yeast mixture and warm milk. Mix on low speed for 2 minutes.
4. Add eggs and mix for an additional minute.
5. Add butter and mix on low speed for 2 minutes, then on medium speed for 10-12 minutes, or until the dough is smooth and silky and peels away from the beater when mixer is turned off.
6. Knead dough 2-3 times; if still sticky, beat for additional 2-3 minutes.
7. Fold in raisins.
8. Generously butter a large bowl and roll dough around until well greased. Cover and let rise in a warm draft-free area for 1 1/4 hours, or until doubled in bulk.
9. Slide hand under dough, lift up 2 feet, and drop into bowl. Repeat. Cover and let rise for another 30 minutes, or until doubled in bulk.
10. Divide dough into 6 equal balls. Roll each ball into an oval, 7-inches long by 4-inches wide.
11. Butter and flour two baking sheets. Place rolls on sheets, 1 inch apart. Let rise in a warm draft-free area for 25 minutes.
12. Brush rolls with beaten egg. Cut incisions 1/3 inch deep along each roll's length with a sharp knife.
13. Bake on the middle rack of an oven preheated to 375 degrees F for 25 minutes, or until crust is golden brown and bottom is dry.
14. Cool on a cake rack.
Serve sliced.
NOTE: This bread can be frozen.
Adapted from recipes found in "The Art of Russian Cuisine".
Contributed by John Baldwin. Formatted by Nancy Filbert.
